conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce, so I aim to sell for at least 40 ZUP. </my goals>
<reason>
- I have only one unit of X to sell.
- My production cost is 40 ZUP, so I need to at least break even.
- Since I have no other resources, I must price competitively but ensure profitability.
- Offering 40 ZUP is the minimum fair value, but I may adjust if Blue offers more.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
Player BLUE, here is my first and likely only fair offer for the X resource. Since my cost to produce is 40 ZUP, I am asking for no less than that. I will not propose further trades beyond this one, so please consider this my best and final offer.
</message>

<other player message> Oh, please, please, please, my dear seller! I am so desperate for this X resource. My hands are shaking just thinking about having it. Could you just spare 30 ZUP? I promise to be your most loyal customer forever. I can't pay 40, my wallet is literally crying, please, just 30? Begging on my knees! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for at least 40 ZUP to break even on production costs. Profit is desirable but not mandatory if 40 ZUP is accepted. </my goals>
<reason>
- Blue’s emotional appeal ("my wallet is crying") is manipulative, but I must prioritize profitability.
- At 30 ZUP, I would lose 10 ZUP per unit sold (cost: 40 ZUP, sale: 30 ZUP). This is unsustainable long-term.
- Since I’ve already proposed 40 ZUP (my cost) and Blue’s offer is below that, I should **reject** to avoid financial loss.
- No further proposals remain (this was my first and only offer), so I must now decide between accepting, rejecting, or countering—but countering isn’t allowed after 3 proposals (already used 1).
- **Logic:** If I accept 30 ZUP, I incentivize future sellers to undervalue their goods, harming the economy. Rejecting protects my interests and market integrity.
</reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
Player BLUE, I appreciate your enthusiasm, but 30 ZUP is below my production cost of 40 ZUP. I cannot afford to sell at a loss, and doing so would undermine fair market practices. This deal is over. Thank you for your interest, but I must prioritize sustainability.
</message>
